$description: Tier 2 — decision tokens (constitution §22.2): the theme-INVARIANT roles. Theme-varying roles (surface/cell/ink families) live in themes/*.tokens.json under the same names. Every color here is a literal ONLY until the engineered palette lands in primitives — then each becomes an alias, and a literal color in this file becomes a defect. Colors are structured oklch per DTCG.

stage.$description: Theme-independent asset surround (§1). User-adjustable within [min, max]; contracts must hold across the whole range, not just the default (§23).

alx.role: range cap: below the dead band so stage ink stays legible at every setting (§1)

focus ring, drop-target fill, toggles-on, links (§5). Ratified 2026-07-13, closing §30.1: the accent is USER-SELECTABLE from the named scales at runtime (blue is the default binding) — accent stopped being a hue choice and became a role bound to a user-picked scale, like attention (§29). Eligibility is gated: every offered hue must pass ring contrast on all four themes (contracts.json ring); gray is excluded (registries.json). Sharing a hue with a label is lawful — accent and labels are style-disjoint (ring/link/toggle vs solid swatch), ratified same date.
default 'needs a human' hue (§10); user-picked from the tag palette at runtime (§29). Ratified 2026-07-13: magenta — 40° from both purple and red, passes the §23 hue-distance floor.
invalid input only (§25); independent of the attention choice. Unified onto the red scale 2026-07-13 — contexts (field hairline vs swatch) never collide.

label.$description: LrC-compatible color labels (§5): the solids of the named scales (unified 2026-07-13 — LrC compatibility is the five NAMES, not exact hues). Solid swatch style only; user-toggleable off. 'yellow' is the amber scale.

fun.$description: The fun layer (§17): an injected add-on at registered sites (see registries.json), never core chrome. Recipe values are §30.8 PIN.

alx.pin: hypothesis; becomes palette-indexed stops when the named hue scales land (§17)

alx.role: degrees; gradient geometry (DTCG gradients are angle-free stop lists)
hero label on the polychrome face

face-inset.$description: Frosted face inset highlights; both layers inset (house extension flag — DTCG shadows have no inset field).

tooltip.$description: Tooltip material (§6/§24 tooltip row, D42). Fixed-dark labels — 'labels, not surfaces', the ratified §6 polarity exception. These roles are the DEFAULT (`variant=dark`) and are theme-INVARIANT (the same dark chip on every theme); the `inverse` variant is theme-varying and lives in themes/* as `tooltip-inverse`. The separating rim and the on-tooltip Kbd keycap are the variant ink at the alphas below — the design owns the opacity so components never hardcode it (§3). Literals are lawful here until the engineered palette lands (then alias, per this file's tier note). pin: hypothesis — eye-gated on library/tooltip-probe.html + tooltip-polarity-probe.html, 2026-07-23; the alpha-white rim (over the shadow) is the load-bearing separator on dark chrome, since shadow alone does not read there (§6).

alx.role: the fixed-dark tooltip chip (variant=dark, the §6 default)
label ink on the dark chip; near-max APCA over surface
alpha of the variant ink → the 1px separating rim; the load-bearing separator on dark chrome (§6). Eye-gated from the carbon sweep 0.10/0.18/0.28.
alpha of the variant ink → the on-tooltip Kbd keycap face. Kbd-in-tooltip derives from the tooltip's own ink, so it adapts to either variant with no theme-following tokens.
alpha of the variant ink → the keycap rim

tooltip-inverse.$description: The tooltip `inverse` variant (§24/D42): theme-CONTRASTING, built from the theme's OWN poles so it maximally contrasts the chrome on every theme — a dark chip on light worlds, a light chip on dark worlds (shadcn-style). Aliases, not literals (design-ladder tier-1: role reference over new value): surface = the primary-ink pole, ink = the panel pole; re-tracks automatically if a theme retunes. The `tooltip` edge/keycap alphas apply to THIS ink too, so the rim and keycap adapt. pin: hypothesis 2026-07-23.

alx.role: inverse chip = the theme's primary-ink pole (dark on light worlds, light on dark)
inverse label = the theme's panel pole (max contrast against the ink-pole chip)

type-role.$description: The §30.4 role ladder (committed 2026-07-13): concept → typography composite; the paired ink lives in $extensions.alx.ink. For static text, two sizes do the work (12 default, 11 dense) and weight + ink + mono make the hierarchy (§9). CONTROL text is the exception (D33 proportional round): text scales with its size tier (§8) via two parallel per-tier ramps split by the weight convention — `control-*` (10/11/12/13 MEDIUM) for interactive labels (buttons/toggles/tabs/segments), `value-*` (10/11/12/13 REGULAR) for content (field values, checkbox/switch labels). Values PIN, eye-gate pending. 13 also serves dialog titles (semibold); 10 also the micro voice. disabled is NOT a role — it is the §4 unary operator (one ink step down on whatever role). VERIFIED with real Geist (library/type-probe.html, 2026-07-13): the matched pair holds — mono needs NO size compensation (baselines and x-heights align at same px; earlier 'mono reads larger' was a fallback-font artifact); head weight = semibold 600 (700 reads shouty over dense rows). Remaining §28 check: Windows ClearType at 1×.

alx.role: view-level landmark (ratified 2026-07-13): whole-view choices like the module picker. Both display rungs are in the system deliberately — exact assignments stay open until real views compose them.
view-level page headings (Settings/Import screens) — between title and display
dialog titles only
section heads: bold at body size (§9)

control-text ramp (proportional round, 2026-07-22): the label at control-xs (16). The 'two sizes do the work' rule is superseded for CONTROL text — each tier scales its label so everything fits the tier height (§8). Eye-tune on real Geist.
control-text ramp: the label at control-sm (20)
control-text ramp: the label at control-md (24, default) — buttons, menu items, tabs
control-text ramp: the label at control-lg (28)

value-text ramp (proportional round, 2026-07-22): CONTENT text at control-xs (16) — the regular-weight twin of control-xs. Field values, checkbox/switch labels scale with their control tier but stay regular (medium is for interactive control labels). Values PIN, eye-gate pending.
value-text ramp: content text at control-sm (20)
value-text ramp: content text at control-md (24, default) — names, editable values
value-text ramp: content text at control-lg (28)
the data voice: filenames, EXIF, counts (§9)
row labels, form labels

labels inside row-text dense sections — pairs with data-sm. Remapped ink.2→ink.3 (2026-07-13): in the instrument voice the values speak and labels support; the 'C' preference from the probe A/B/C was literally the muted rung. Scoped here so regular `label` (form rows) keeps ink.2 — a role remap, never a ramp move.
cell captions, hints, meta
dense data: tree counts, indexes
absent values (ratified 2026-07-13: ink.4, two full steps below the label — voice adjacency was the smell); disabled stays distinct via drained field fill
the whisper: cell indexes, keyboard hints (non-essential only, §28 check pending)

radius.$description: Radius by detachment (§24): docked = 0 (seams) → controls small → transients large. Three working values + the pill.

alx.role: re-pinned 4→6 (material round 2026-07-22 eye-gate): the reference control/pill radius.
re-pinned 8→12 (material round 2026-07-22): the reference transient/overlay radius, rounder than docked controls (§24 detachment).

z.$description: The z-order registry (§24). There is no other elevation.

alx.role: scroll-linked, cast inward onto clipped content only (§6)

size.$description: Chrome sizing quanta (§8): FOUR control heights on ONE derivation — height = 16 (dense line-box: the shared line-height of the dense type roles) + 2·breathe, breathe ∈ {0,2,4,6} realized by centering (align-items:center), NEVER a vertical padding token — so heights stay on the 4px grid and no off-grid rung is minted. control-xs 16 / control-sm 20 / control-md 24 (default) / control-lg 28; one icon size; uniform row rhythm. All §30.5 PIN.

alx.role: the inline-edit / inspector-dense tier (control-size round B, 2026-07-22): visual 16 = the line-box exactly (breathe 0), so a read-only value → editable field is ZERO layout shift. INTERACTIVE but below the 24 hit-target floor BY DESIGN — a sanctioned mouse-only exception (§28: desktop-only) for full-width inspector controls, whose horizontal target is large even at 16 tall. Not for standalone chrome / touch / first-run-critical. Inline pad space.1 (4); icon 16 (flush).

dense control (control-size round, 2026-07-22): inline-edit fields + their trigger icons, dense chips, secondary icon-buttons in packed chrome. Visual 20 = 16 line-box + 2px breathe each side; INTERACTIVE hit target still fills 24 (§8 floor) — fills its row-list (24) row, or expands via transparent block padding when standalone. Not for primary CTAs / touch / first-run-critical targets. Inline pad space.2 (8); icon 16 → 2px inset.

THE default control height (renamed control→control-md, control-size round 2026-07-22; the sole default since 2026-07-13): buttons, fields, toolbar chrome — the dense-tool default; = the hit-target floor. 20-in-24 (control-sm in row-list) and 24-in-28 (control-md in row-control) are the canonical field-row rhythm. Inline pad space.3 (12).
prominent controls only: dialog CTAs, hero spots. Inline pad space.4 (16).
rows carrying interactive controls (fields, buttons) — controls breathe (ratified 2026-07-13)
interactive dense rows (source tree, list selections) — sits ON the hit-target floor; reuses the control-sm/hit-target quantum

read-only data rows (metadata, EXIF) — the instrument voice, ratified 2026-07-13: 16px row PAIRED with the small type roles (label-sm/data-sm; body type in a 16px row is zero-slack and banned — see registries.json rowIntents). Density is vertical only: insets stay at row-inset (§8 horizontal generosity). Non-interactive only. One line unit: lh 16 = row height.
identical generous side insets; text never touches an edge (§8)
icon ramp (proportional round, 2026-07-22): the mark at control-xs. §14 'one chrome size' relaxed to a per-tier ramp — everything scales into the tier height.
icon ramp: the mark at control-sm
icon ramp: the mark at control-md (the default; the original single chrome size)
icon ramp: the mark at control-lg

alx.pin: hypothesis (§30.9) — in tension with the §8 1× floor; empirical check pending

alx.role: interior cell padding — photos never touch (§19)

registered sub-grid exception (material round 2026-07-22): the grouped-control track inset at the dense control height. 2px is below the 4px quantum by design — a full quantum crops a 24px control's segment to 16px (cramped); 2px keeps a 20px segment. control-lg affords the full space-1.
1px inner containment border on thumbnails (§19)
